Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (creatinine slightly elevated). Urinalysis shows proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, elevated bilirubin, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible renal or hepatic dysfunction and/or urinary tract pathology. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—may indicate analytical error or altered red cell turnover. Recommend clinical correlation, repeat HbA1c, and further evaluation for renal and hepatic disease.
